front.title: HJS: An Accountability Layer for AI Agents

abstract.t: This document defines the Human Judgment Structure (HJS), a minimal and infrastructure‑grade accountability layer for AI agents, built on the Judgment Event Protocol (JEP) [draft-wang-jep-judgment-event-protocol-01]. HJS provides a cryptographic mechanism to manage and verify responsibility for AI agent judgment behaviors across heterogeneous platforms. Its core design consists of four primitives (Judge, Delegate, Terminate, Verify) inherited from JEP, a minimal receipt structure with responsibility chaining, and a three‑tier privacy architecture for accountability governance. All advanced features (state machines, compliance proofs, governance mechanisms) are defined as optional extensions or informative appendices, preserving the structure’s narrow‑waist stability and compatibility with JEP.
